root_text.sn22.45:1.5: yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.45:1.6: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passato cittaṁ virajjati vimuccati anupādāya āsavehi.
root_text.sn22.45:1.13: yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.45:1.14: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passato cittaṁ virajjati vimuccati anupādāya āsavehi.
root_text.sn22.45:1.15: Rūpadhātuyā ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cittaṁ virattaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti anupādāya āsavehi, vedanādhātuyā …pe…
root_text.sn22.45:1.18: viññāṇadhātuyā ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cittaṁ virattaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti anupādāya āsavehi.
root_text.sn22.45:1.19: Vimuttattā ṭhitaṁ. Ṭhitattā santusitaṁ. Santusitattā na paritassati. Aparitassaṁ paccattaññeva parinibbāyati.
root_text.sn22.45:1.20: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.45:1.5: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.45:1.6: Seeing truly with right understanding like this, the mind becomes dispassionate and freed from defilements by not grasping.
translation_text.sn22.45:1.13: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.45:1.14: Seeing truly with right understanding like this, the mind becomes dispassionate and freed from defilements by not grasping.
translation_text.sn22.45:1.15: If a mendicant’s mind is dispassionate towards the form element, the feeling element,
translation_text.sn22.45:1.18: and the consciousness element, it’s freed from defilements by not grasping.
translation_text.sn22.45:1.19: Being free, it’s stable. Being stable, it’s content. Being content, they’re not anxious. Not being anxious, they personally become extinguished.
translation_text.sn22.45:1.20: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

reference_text.sn22.45:1.1: ms13S3_199, msdiv45, ndp13.277, pts-vp-pli3.45, sya17.57, vri25.42
